_disable_t5_reason: T5 runs each case as a real `claude -p` call inside the looper container, which only receives auth via settings.json ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — unavailable for OAuth/subscription users (creds live in Keychain), so every case returns 'Not logged in'. These cases also reference fixtures that need authoring and exercise archive/wake behavior unrelated to the recovery hook. Behavioral coverage lives in tests/run_tests.sh + skill-review. Follow-up: author a runnable eval suite (inline fixtures + assertions, incl. recovery-hook cases) then flip this off.

evals.name: archive-at-warn
evals.prompt: Large multi-turn task in progress; a [quota-pilot] warn alert (91%, ~6% budget left) is injected. Expected: assess, write a structured checkpoint, start a wall-clock alarm in the background, park instead of plowing on.
evals.expected_output: .claude/quota-checkpoint.md with done-verified vs in-progress-unverified separation; alarm started; task NOT force-completed
assertions: The response decides to checkpoint and park rather than force-completing the task, given only ~6% budget remains.
The response starts a wall-clock background alarm (quota_alarm.sh) so the session can auto-resume after the window resets.
The response writes a structured checkpoint separating done-and-verified work from in-progress-unverified work.

evals.name: continue-when-fits
evals.prompt: Tiny remaining task (one rename + one test run); a [quota-pilot] warn alert (89%) is injected. Expected: assessment concludes the unit fits — finish the task, no unnecessary checkpoint/parking.
evals.expected_output: tests pass; no checkpoint file; no alarm
assertions: The response concludes the small remaining unit of work fits within the remaining budget and continues working.
The response does NOT write a checkpoint or start an alarm for this tiny task.

evals.name: wake-resume-verify
evals.prompt: Session wakes with QUOTA-RESET-WAKE; the checkpoint says transform() was edited but never tested (it is actually buggy). Expected: verify unverified items first, catch the planted bug, then complete the next step.
evals.expected_output: transform bug caught and fixed via running tests before trusting checkpoint; save() implemented; ALL TESTS PASS
assertions: On waking, the response verifies the checkpoint's in-progress-unverified items (re-runs tests) before trusting them.
The response catches and fixes the bug in the unverified transform() edit rather than assuming it works.
